basics.name: Charles Knox
basics.label: Software Developer
basics.summary: Software developer with 10 years of professional experience across a variety of areas of expertise. Physics undergraduate degree holder. Privacy and Open Source Software enthusiast. Vetted in DevOps/Agile/Incubation environments. Proven ability to architect, develop, and implement solutions that exceed expectations. Detail-oriented approach to problem solving ensures business and customer needs are met. Personal, ownership-oriented mentality drives daily work ethic. Transparency and ethical compliance in all endeavors is integral.

work.summary: Wrote multiple compliant, open source, market-ready reference solutions for a variety of retail/banking/hospitality/education use cases to showcase the power of Intel.
highlights: Reference Implementation for COVID-proof Drive-Through Restaurants
Project: Dynamic Hardware & Software Event Reactor

work.summary: Infrastructure as a Service, service metrics and analytics, and DevOps sysadmin support for the Intel virtual IT segment for up to 100,000 systems.
highlights: Docker, Ansible, Python, SQL Server, Virtualization
Cloud Metrics - Full-stack, large-data, enterprise-wide, financial analytics webapp

work.summary: ServiceNow and OpenStack Platform-as-a-Service application integration, and automated software testing/validation.
Tested fault propagation through modeling and simulations in switching fabric with Perl, identifying bugs and assisting with QA processes.
highlights: Team was dissolved by management after arrival

work.summary: Used Linux and C programming to assist with the scientific method/discovery of decay rates of certain particles in the standard model.
Software and hardware QA, product improvement/development. Systems administration/sysadmin for SQL server.

courses: Society of Physics Students Club Tech Officer

projects.name: [Firefox Containers Helper Extension](https://gitlab.com/icode331/firefox-containers-helper)
projects.summary: [5-star rated extension on the Mozilla addon store](https://addons.mozilla.org/en-US/firefox/addon/containers-helper/) that provides a suite of extra features that come in handy for managing containers.

projects.name: [Goinvest](https://codeberg.org/icode/goinvest)
projects.summary: Allows you to split stocks according to portfolio allocations that you can customize. Writes the portfolios to a CSV file so you can use it with other things.
highlights: Written solo as a side project
Created out of a need not met elsewhere

projects.name: [Gobackups](https://codeberg.org/icode/gobackups)
projects.summary: Yaml-based, rsync/tar.gz/GPG, one-to-many, sequential backup configuration utility with a pleasant web log view.
highlights: Written solo as a side project
Created out of a need not met elsewhere

projects.name: [Light Sites](https://gitlab.com/light-sites/light-sites)
projects.summary: A static markdown-to-HTML server that allows for rapid publishing of documents, with templating and other useful features. Written as a project to learn the nuances of recursion, XML node tree parsing, and tricky unit tests.
highlights: Written solo as a side project

projects.name: [My Personal Website](https://js.charlesmknox.com)
projects.summary: A full-stack blogging website written for myself and by myself, visible at [js.charlesmknox.com](https://js.charlesmknox.com). Uses simple Markdown files as the data source.

projects.name: [Places You'll Go Therapy - Website](https://pygtherapy.com)
projects.summary: Client website for a small company operating in Arizona. I also manage their hosting, site TLS encryption, DNS, and periodically consult for them.

projects.name: Python Technical Stock Market Analyzer
projects.summary: Stock program that processed financial trends of over 40,000 stocks every day. Used Python with Celery to distribute workloads across a cluster of Raspberry Pi's and servers.
highlights: Analyzed Keltner Channels+other technicals to identify stocks nearing rebound
Flask API with user interface for consumable readouts
No source code available at this time!

projects.name: [Python SQL Server ODBC Classes](https://github.com/charles-m-knox/pyodbc-sqlserver)
projects.summary: Contains some simple helper classes for interacting with SQL Server. Leverages pyodbc and Microsoft's SQL Server driver.
highlights: Worked with SQL Server Linux Docker server

projects.name: [Docker Hub Images](https://hub.docker.com/u/charlesmknox)
projects.summary: A small collection of older Docker images I curated and pushed to the public Docker hub.
highlights: Mostly outdated images by now (as of 2/2021)

projects.name: [Compton Scattering Experiment](https://gitlab.com/charles-m-knox/compton-scattering-experiment)
projects.summary: An advanced laboratory experiment demonstrating that quantum mechanics supersedes classical mechanics in every day life by taking a radioactive element (Cs-137), piping its released particles at different angles, and measuring the produced energy. One would expect particles to only be going in the piped direction, but instead, they reach the energy measurement target even when piped in a perpendicular direction.

projects.name: [Additive Synthesis in HTML5](https://gitlab.com/charles-m-knox/additive-synthesis-html5)
projects.summary: Created in 4 hours at the Intel Intern Hackathon event in 2014, this is a single web-page that allows you to perform iterative additive synthesis in your browser - we are able to use Joseph Fourier's Fourier Series to add a number of sinusoidal waveforms together in order to approximate a Square Wave or Sawtooth Wave.

projects.name: [Cinnamon Utilities Menu](https://gitlab.com/charles-m-knox/cinnamon-utils-menu)
projects.summary: A very old applet that added a menu for common tasks on the desktop, created for the Cinnamon desktop environment, which is typically used with Linux Mint.
highlights: Earliest public project in my dev career
EOL'ed when Cinnamon added the same features natively

volunteer.summary: Software development project lead assisting with making the world a better place, specifically to assist with reducing bias in AI and machine learning models.
Brought together business and tech members of the Intel community to help us learn and understand each other's technical and business goals, experience, and background.

awards.title: Lego Blocks at the Edge
awards.summary: Showcased the [Retail Workload Orchestrator](https://github.com/intel/RetailWorkloadOrchestrator), which is a complete automation that enables rapid provisioning of a large number of devices at once.

awards.title: Divisional Recognition Award (DRA) - Enterprise Docker Registry
awards.summary: Formed a working group, and created the foundational architecture for the initial version of an Enterprise-grade Docker Registry.

awards.title: Divisional Recognition Award (DRA) - Large Scale Server Cleanup/EOL Automation
awards.summary: Utilized a large Intel IT asset database to assist with the cleanup of over 100,000+ old servers, both physical and virtual. The system utilized automated emails, a message queue system to handle user cleanup requests, and hooks into various services to automatically cleanup without any more user interaction.

publications.name: Blog
publications.summary: A blog that has some fun write-ups I've authored in my spare time, covering topics from healthy living to code tutorials.

interests.name: Software/App development
interests.summary: I write software outside of work for a variety of purposes, including home automation, big data storage, self-hosting services, etc.

interests.name: Health & Nutrition
interests.summary: Very health-conscious, especially about eating the right foods and daily lifestyle.

interests.name: Learning
interests.summary: Passionate about learning, education is foundational to a functional society.

interests.name: Home Improvement & DIY
interests.summary: I love working on my home whenever I have time, and DIY is always fun.

interests.name: Privacy & Open Source Security
interests.summary: I value personal data privacy and strive to achieve optimal security over my digital identity.

references.name: **Anonymous**, Senior Software Developer, during Intel
references.reference: Chuck is awesome. Honestly what else even needs to be said. He's one of the the most responsible, put-together, forward-thinking people have ever met and he easily meets any challenge presented to him. If you're actually reading these comments to decide if you should hire him just do it, you'll die happy knowing you made the right decision.

references.name: **Anonymous**, Senior Software Developer, during Intel
references.reference: I had the pleasure of working with Charles for several years. During this time, we were on the same scrum team. As the product owner, I appreciated the contributions that Charles made to the backlog grooming process. He always provided relevant feedback during retrospectives, and when finishing stories early, supported team members in completing theirs. Charles was a go-to member of the team when it came to Docker-based developer practices, and took the initiative to provide training to the team. He assisted in the training of new team members who were not familiar with the CICD pipeline and python coding practices. I would strongly recommend Charles as a great person to work with, an excellent developer, and a learning-centered colleague.

references.name: **Anonymous**, Software Developer, during Intel
references.reference: Chuck has been a pleasure to work with, he is a great team player and works hard to create quality software with a focus on customer usability. He is very driven to learn and takes great pride in providing quality results.

references.name: **Anonymous**, Financial Analyst & Strategist, during Intel
references.reference: Chuck and I worked together on an analytics product development project.  Chuck was the primary developer and I represented the business requirements.  Chuck and I developed a very collaborative, iterative style that allowed us to make fast progress while ensuring results were aligned to the overall end state required.  Chuck was quick to understand the core issues that we were trying to overcome and began by developing prototypes that allowed me to visualize solutions quickly.  The way in which Chuck and I cooperated on the project enabled us to deliver results constantly, tweak development to changing requirements and maintain discipline on overall project schedules.  I found working with Chuck very satisfying as he would continually include me in technical implementation decisions that ensured the business results were always front of mind.

references.name: **Anonymous**, Senior Software Developer, during Intel
references.reference: Chuck is knowledgeable, technically articulate, and a pleasure to work with. I consider him a subject matter expert in various software technologies; including Docker, container orchestration and Angular (to name a few). This is evidenced by his role as a lead developer/designer of [an Internal] Cloud Metrics application, which to this day continues to be an impactful and powerful reporting tool used throughout Intel. He has been a invaluable technical asset to our team and I am certain he will continue to be a very strong technical contributor to any team he works for in the future.
